Assignment For Session 8:
The Big Problem
1. Create your own Big Problem within your VSL template
(comes after your Vital Connection section) using the
formula below:
The Big Problem
The Conspiracy Cycle
Open Loop To Step 4
Part 1: The Conspiracy Cycle
The Big Lie
1. Find your Big Lie by going back through the “without” exercise in
Part 3 of your Snap Suggestion assignment. Anything you
labeled as a “without” can be used as a Big Lie. For example:
“Lose all the weight you want (benefit) without starving yourself
skinny (lie).
2. You can use up to 3 Big Lies.
Transition Into The Lie(s)
1. Build a transition slide or series of slides that introduces the
viewer to the concept that they’ve “been lied to.” For example:

-- 1 of 4 --

“The real problem is the fact that you’ve been lied to for years
when it comes to (your reader’s desire or need.)”
Describe The Lie
1. Simply describe the lie. If you plan to use more than one lie, be sure
to word the slide appropriately. For example:
“One of the most common lies people believe is that you have to
starve yourself in order to get skinny.” OR:
“Now, perhaps at one time in your life, maybe even now, you
thought you had to starve yourself in order to lose weight…”
Label It As False
1. Here, you simply declare the lie to be just that—a lie.
Example:
“That’s just a big, fat lie…” OR:
“Nothing could be further from the truth…”
Results Of Believing The Lie
1. Paint a vivid picture of what the prospect’s life will be like in
the form of a mini Nightmare Story.
2. Be sure this is worded in the present tense.
3. See Presentation For Tuesday, December 6th Call—Download Prior
To Class for slide examples.
Not What I Want For you
1. Simply state this any way you see fit. Example:
“This is not what I want for you… and I know it’s not what you
desire for yourself…”

-- 2 of 4 --

It’s Not Your Fault
Allow the prospect off the psychological hook by assuring him/her that
this lie is to blame for their problem. This allows the prospect to feel
more confidence in his/her ability to make a decision during the Grand
Offer section. Example:
“This is just one of the many reasons that your struggle to (prospect’s
desire) is not your fault…” OR:
“It really anger me that we’ve been told this lie for so long… and let
me tell you, it’s not your fault if this has held you back from (prospect’s
desire.)
Blame This
1. “If you really want to blame something, blame _____.” Here you
begin to build the conspiracy. See presentation slide PDF for
specific examples.
2. Next, explain why______ is to blame.
3. Finally, provide proof that ______ is to blame (if possible.)
The Big Truth
Correct the lie by using the truths presented in one of your 3 Tips. See
presentation slide PDF for specific examples.
Part 2: Open The Loop To Bigger Solution
1. Begin by stating that the reader “now knows the truth”—that
these lies have been holding them back from achieving their
goal.
2. “Let’s dive into 3 (or however many) tips so we can get you on
the right track to discovering (prospect’s desired objective.)”
3. The most important of these tips is (your How-To Tip,
preferably with a coined phrase. Remember, this is built around
your primary benefit’s driving force. Example:
“The most important of these 3 tips is my caloric staggering

-- 3 of 4 --

principle… And I’ll cover that in just a moment…”
Stating that you’ll cover the tip later opens the loop to The Bigger
Solution.

-- 4 of 4 --
